branches.label: Quantitative Aptitude
branches.detail: Tests numerical ability across arithmetic, algebra, geometry, trigonometry and data interpretation. Usually 25 questions in Tier 1, with deeper coverage in Tier 2. Speed and accuracy in calculation matter as much as concept clarity.

children.label: Arithmetic
children.detail: The highest-weightage Quant topic — covers percentages, profit/loss, time-work, and interest calculations that recur in almost every SSC paper.

children.label: Percentage
children.detail: Converting fractions and ratios to percentages and back; the base skill nearly every other Arithmetic question builds on.

children.label: Profit & Loss
children.detail: Calculating cost price, selling price, profit/loss percentage, and discount — frequently combined with percentage questions.

children.label: Time & Work
children.detail: Problems on individual/combined work rates, efficiency, and pipes-and-cisterns variants.

children.label: SI & CI
children.detail: Simple and Compound Interest formulas, including installment and difference-based questions.

children.label: Algebra
children.detail: Equation-solving and identity-based questions, generally easier marks if formulas are memorized well.

children.label: Linear Equations
children.detail: Solving for unknowns in one or two variables, including word problems converted into equations.

children.label: Quadratic Equations
children.detail: Finding roots via factorization or the quadratic formula, and forming equations from given roots.

children.label: Geometry
children.detail: Properties of shapes and figures — a visually intensive topic that rewards diagram practice over rote memorization.

children.label: Triangles
children.detail: Congruence, similarity, Pythagoras theorem, and centroid/incenter/circumcenter properties.

children.label: Circles
children.detail: Chord, tangent, and angle properties, including cyclic quadrilaterals.

children.label: Mensuration
children.detail: Area, perimeter, and volume formulas for 2D and 3D shapes — a guaranteed few marks if formulas are memorized.

children.label: Trigonometry
children.detail: Standard angle values and identities applied to height-and-distance word problems.

children.label: Identities
children.detail: Core trig identities (sin²+cos²=1 and variants) used to simplify expressions in MCQ format.

children.label: Heights & Distances
children.detail: Real-world angle-of-elevation/depression problems solved using trigonometric ratios.

children.label: Data Interpretation
children.detail: Reading and calculating from graphs and tables — tests speed more than concept difficulty.

children.label: Bar Graphs
children.detail: Comparing values across categories shown as bars, often requiring percentage or ratio calculations on the data.

children.label: Pie Charts
children.detail: Reading proportions from a circular chart, usually converting degree/percentage splits into actual values.

branches.label: Reasoning
branches.detail: General Intelligence & Reasoning tests logical thinking through verbal, non-verbal, and analytical question types. Typically the most scoring section if practiced regularly since it relies on pattern recognition rather than memorized facts.

children.label: Verbal
children.detail: Word- and number-based logical patterns.

children.label: Analogy
children.detail: Identifying the relationship between a given word/number pair and applying it to a new pair.

children.label: Classification
children.detail: Picking the odd one out from a group of words, numbers, or figures based on a shared rule.

children.label: Series
children.detail: Completing number or letter sequences by identifying the underlying pattern.

children.label: Non-Verbal
children.detail: Figure and image-based reasoning that tests spatial visualization.

children.label: Mirror Images
children.detail: Predicting how a figure appears when reflected, a common source of careless errors under time pressure.

children.label: Paper Folding
children.detail: Visualizing cuts/punches on a folded paper and how they appear when unfolded.

children.label: Logical
children.detail: Deductive reasoning questions based on statements and given conditions.

children.label: Syllogism
children.detail: Drawing valid conclusions from two or more given statements using set/Venn diagram logic.

children.label: Blood Relations
children.detail: Tracing family relationships from descriptive clues, often requiring a quick family-tree sketch.

children.label: Coding-Decoding
children.detail: Decrypting letter/number substitution patterns to decode or encode given words.

branches.label: English
branches.detail: Tests grammar, vocabulary, and comprehension. Scoring well here depends on consistent reading habits and active vocabulary building rather than last-minute cramming.

children.label: Grammar
children.detail: Rule-based sentence correction and structure questions.

children.label: Tenses
children.detail: Correct usage of verb forms across past, present, and future, including common SSC trap sentences.

children.label: Articles
children.detail: Correct use of a/an/the, including cases where no article is needed.

children.label: Voice Change
children.detail: Converting sentences between active and passive voice while preserving meaning.

children.label: Vocabulary
children.detail: Word meaning and usage, best built through regular reading rather than isolated word lists.

children.label: Synonyms
children.detail: Choosing words with the closest meaning to a given word from the options.

children.label: Antonyms
children.detail: Choosing words with opposite meaning — frequently paired with synonym questions in the same set.

children.label: Idioms
children.detail: Recognizing the intended meaning of common English idiomatic phrases.

children.label: Comprehension
children.detail: Passage-based questions testing reading speed and inference ability.

children.label: Reading Passage
children.detail: Answering direct and inferential questions based on a given paragraph or article.

children.label: Cloze Test
children.detail: Filling blanks in a passage with the most contextually appropriate word.

branches.label: General Awareness
branches.detail: Static GK and current affairs spanning history, polity, geography, and science. The hardest section to predict, so broad, consistent reading beats targeted last-minute revision.

children.label: History
children.detail: Indian history from ancient civilizations through the independence movement.

children.label: Ancient India
children.detail: Indus Valley Civilization, Vedic period, and major ancient dynasties.

children.label: Freedom Struggle
children.detail: Key events, movements, and leaders from the Indian independence struggle (1857–1947).

children.label: Polity
children.detail: The structure and functioning of the Indian government as defined by the Constitution.

children.label: Constitution
children.detail: Structure, schedules, amendments, and the constituent assembly's drafting process.

children.label: Fundamental Rights
children.detail: The six fundamental rights guaranteed under Articles 12–35 and their exceptions.

children.label: Geography
children.detail: Physical and political geography of India and the world.

children.label: Physical Geography
children.detail: Landforms, climate, and natural resource distribution across India and globally.

children.label: Indian Rivers
children.detail: Major river systems, their origins, tributaries, and the states/cities they pass through.

children.label: Science
children.detail: NCERT-level general science across physics, chemistry, and biology.

children.label: Physics Basics
children.detail: Fundamental laws, units, and everyday-application physics questions at school level.

children.label: Biology Basics
children.detail: Human body systems, diseases, and basic plant/animal biology at school level.

children.label: Current Affairs
children.detail: Recent national and international developments, usually covering the last 6–12 months before the exam.

children.label: Govt Schemes
children.detail: Central government welfare and policy schemes, their objectives, and launch details.

children.label: Awards
children.detail: Major national and international awards, their categories, and recent recipients.
